channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US and Philippines sign a pact to secure shared military intelligence and weapons technology
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The United States and the Philippines signed an agreement on Monday to secure the exchange of highly confidential military intelligence and technology in key weapons the U.S. would provide to Manila.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sri Lanka's Marxist-leaning president appoints Cabinet after election win
item.description: COLOMBO, Sri Lanka (AP) — Sri Lanka’s new Marxist-leaning president swore in a 22-member Cabinet on Monday after his party secured a two-thirds parliamentary majority in last week’s election.
item.dc:creator: BHARATHA MALLAWARACHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Climate talks in Azerbaijan head into their second week, coinciding with G20 in Rio
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — United Nations talks on getting money to curb and adapt to climate change resumed Monday with tempered hope that negotiators and ministers can work through disagreements and hammer out a deal after slow progress last week.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, MELINA WALLING and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Young people, whose futures are at stake in UN climate talks, push through anger to fight for hope
item.description: As leaders and negotiators grapple with big questions of money and power, youth COP attendees and activists from around the world grapple with their own questions about how much their voices matter.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Typhoon Man-yi leaves 7 dead in landslide in Philippines and worsens crisis from back-to-back storms
item.description: Typhoon Man-yi has left at least seven people dead in a landslide, destroyed scores of houses and displaced large numbers of villagers before blowing away from the northern Philippines, worsening the crisis wreaked by multiple back-to-back storms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: French farmers mobilize for protests over EU-Mercosur trade deal
item.description: French farmers are mobilizing for widespread protests Monday, targeting the controversial EU-Mercosur trade agreement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Some Arab Americans who voted for Trump are concerned about his picks for key positions
item.description: Some Arab Americans are expressing concern over Donald Trump's picks for key positions in his next administration.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A makeshift memorial grows in Ukraine’s capital after 1,000 days of war
item.description: After 1,000 days of war, a makeshift memorial has grown in the heart of Kyiv with thousands of blue-and-yellow flags honoring soldiers who died defending Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brazil hosts a G20 summit overshadowed by wars and Trump's return, aiming for a deal to fight hunger
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — With Brazil preparing to host the Group of 20 summit, it appears unlikely the leading rich and developing nations will sign on to a meaningful declaration regarding geopolitics: The meeting Monday and Tuesday in Rio de Janeiro is overshadowed by two major wars and Donald Trump'
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES and MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 45 pro-democracy activists face sentencing in Hong Kong. Here's who some of them are
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Dozens of prominent Hong Kong pro-democracy activists are scheduled to be sentenced Tuesday in the largest case under a national security law that critics say crushed political activism in the semi-autonomous Chinese city.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korean leader calls for expanding his nuclear forces in the face of alleged US threats
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un renewed his call for a “limitless” expansion of his military nuclear program to counter U.S.-led threats in comments reported Monday that were his first direct criticism toward Washington since Donald Trump’s win in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden has authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside Russia: Reports
item.description: President Biden authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied missiles to strike further into Russia, multiple reports said. The move comes as about 10,000 North Korean troops aid Russia in its war and expect to fight in Ukraine in the “next several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Biden becomes the first sitting US president to visit the Amazon rainforest
item.description: Joe Biden on Sunday became the first sitting American president to set foot in the Amazon rainforest, as the incoming Trump administration seems poised to scale back the U.S. commitment to combating climate change.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy: Russia using Iranian weapons in 'massive' attack targeting energy infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian forces say Russia launched over 200 drones and missiles against the nation's power and energy infrastructure this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on flight
item.description: A South Korean man was arrested in Peru for allegedly attempting to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on a flight back to Korea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky says Russia-Ukraine war will end ‘faster’ under Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the Russia-Ukraine war will end “faster” under President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 2,000-year-old Roman road discovered by archaeologists in London
item.description: An ancient Roman road was recently discovered by archaeologists in London. The 2,000-year-old road was located beneath modern-day Old Kent Road.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump looms large as Biden set to meet China's Xi during Latin America summits
item.description: President Biden is in Latin America for the APEC and G-20 summits, in what is likely his last trip as president. The back-to-back summits come as an impending second Trump presidency is near, which may rattle some world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's warfare in Gaza 'consistent with genocide:' UN special committee
item.description: Israel's destruction of vital water, sanitation, and food systems has created a "lethal mix of crises," the report said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senators call for federal probe into Musk's contact with Russia
item.description: A pair of Democratic senators called for a federal investigation into Elon Musk’s involvement in SpaceX’s federal contracts following reports the tech billionaire has been in regular contact with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Iran told Biden administration it won't try to assassinate President-elect Trump: report
item.description: Iran reportedly gave the White House its promise that it will not attempt to kill Donald Trump just weeks before the Justice Department charged a murder-for-hire man in a scheme to kill the former president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protesters storm Putin-backed parliament in Georgia breakaway region
item.description: Protesters in the separatist region of Abkhazia, Georgia stormed the parliament building to demand the region's president step down and reject an investment agreement that could benefit wealthy Russians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump said to lift all military restrictions on Israel on 1st day in office according to reports
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s promise could lead to a 60-day cease-fire, allowing Israel to suspend hostilities until military support resumes under the new administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alex Soros, Jared Kushner reportedly obsessed with Albania
item.description: Alex Soros, whose father is Hungarian, reportedly travels to the capital Tirana so often he has called it “my second home.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats call for Biden to sanction 'radical' Israeli government ministers
item.description: Democrats in the House and Senate are calling on President Biden to sanction two controversial Israeli ministers, saying the two men have promoted violence against Palestinians and are pushing for annexation of territory that undermines U.S. efforts to support a two-state solution.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Moo Deng, Thailand's baby pygmy hippo, gets official song in 4 languages
item.description: In case you can’t get enough of the little pygmy hippo Moo Deng from Thailand, there's now an official song featuring the internet's favorite baby animal — released in four languages for her global fans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's Rubio pick divides Republicans
item.description: Tensions are brewing over President-elect Trump’s move to tap Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to serve as secretary of State, with some of the president-elect’s allies voicing concerns about the pick. The choice has already set into motion the process of Florida Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: 1 dead, Supreme Court evacuated as explosions rattle Brazilian capital
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court was evacuated Wednesday following two explosions in its vicinity, stoking security concerns in the G20's leadup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spain battered by new storm onslaught just weeks after deadly floods
item.description: New storms triggered school closures and transit disruptions in Spain on Wednesday, just two weeks after flash floods left over 220 people dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tourists stranded in Bali for days after Indonesia's volcanic eruption grounds international flights
item.description: Thousands of people were stranded at airports in Indonesia and Australia due to the eruption of Indonesia’s Mount Lewotobi Laki Laki volcano. media reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US warns Russia potentially aiding North Korea's nuclear program in direct threat to Europe, Asia
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken warns that North Korea is benefiting from aiding Russia in its war against Ukraine by potentially aiding Pyongyang's nuclear development, posing a threat for Europe and Asia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driver rams his car into crowd in China, killing 35. Police say he was upset about his divorce
item.description: ZHUHAI, China (AP) — A man who authorities said was upset over his divorce settlement rammed his car into a crowd of people exercising at a sports complex in southern China, killing 35 and severely injuring dozens of others, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: NG HAN GUAN and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US says it will not limit Israel arms transfers after some improvements in flow of aid to Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday that Israel has made some good but limited progress in increasing the flow of humanitarian aid to Gaza and will not limit arms transfers to Israel as it had threatened to a month ago if the situation had not improved.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Church of England head Justin Welby resigns over handling of sex abuse scandal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Archbishop of Canterbury Justin Welby, head of the Church of England and spiritual leader of the global Anglican Communion, resigned Tuesday after an investigation found that he failed to tell police about serial physical and sexual abuse by a volunteer at Christian summer camps as soo
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: President Joe Biden meets with Israel's Isaac Herzog
item.description: Joe Biden met with Israel President Isaac Herzog at the White House on Tuesday, which included a gift from Herzog to Biden.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Jury awards Abu Ghraib detainees $42 million, holds contractor responsible
item.description: ALEXANDRIA, Va. (AP) — A U.S. jury on Tuesday awarded $42 million to three former detainees of Iraq's notorious Abu Ghraib prison, holding a Virginia-based military contractor responsible for contributing to their torture and mistreatment two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT, Associated Press, NewsNation
